name: source-maps
context: Upload source maps to Sentry for readable production stack traces using build plugins or sentry-cli
info: This skill covers source map uploading to Sentry so that minified production stack traces resolve to original source code. There are three upload methods: build tool plugins (@sentry/vite-plugin, @sentry/webpack-plugin, @sentry/rollup-plugin, @sentry/esbuild-plugin) that inject Debug IDs and upload automatically during builds; the sentry-cli for manual uploads in CI pipelines; and the @sentry/wizard for automated setup. Source maps must be associated with a release version that matches the `release` option in `Sentry.init()`. The auth token (SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN) requires 'Project: Read & Write' and 'Release: Admin' scopes. After upload, delete source maps from your deployment to avoid exposing original source code. See [[sentry/setup]] for setting the release identifier and [[sentry/config]] for the release option.
triggers: source maps not working in sentry
body:
# Source Map Uploads

Without source maps, Sentry shows minified stack traces like `a.js:1:42389` instead of `checkout.ts:142 in processPayment`. Source maps bridge the gap between production bundles and original source code.

## Method 1: Build Tool Plugins (Recommended)

Build plugins inject Debug IDs into your bundles and upload source maps automatically during production builds.

### Vite

```bash
npm install @sentry/vite-plugin --save-dev
```

```javascript
// vite.config.js
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { sentryVitePlugin } from "@sentry/vite-plugin";

export default defineConfig({
  build: {
    sourcemap: "hidden", // generate but don't expose in production
  },
  plugins: [
    // Sentry plugin MUST be last
    sentryVitePlugin({
      org: process.env.SENTRY_ORG,
      project: process.env.SENTRY_PROJECT,
      authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,
      sourcemaps: {
        filesToDeleteAfterUpload: ["./**/*.map"],
      },
    }),
  ],
});
```

### Webpack

```bash
npm install @sentry/webpack-plugin --save-dev
```

```javascript
// webpack.config.js
const { sentryWebpackPlugin } = require("@sentry/webpack-plugin");

module.exports = {
  devtool: "source-map",
  plugins: [
    sentryWebpackPlugin({
      org: process.env.SENTRY_ORG,
      project: process.env.SENTRY_PROJECT,
      authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,
    }),
  ],
};
```

### Rollup

```bash
npm install @sentry/rollup-plugin --save-dev
```

```javascript
// rollup.config.js
import { sentryRollupPlugin } from "@sentry/rollup-plugin";

export default {
  output: { sourcemap: "hidden" },
  plugins: [
    sentryRollupPlugin({
      org: process.env.SENTRY_ORG,
      project: process.env.SENTRY_PROJECT,
      authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,
    }),
  ],
};
```

### esbuild

```bash
npm install @sentry/esbuild-plugin --save-dev
```

```javascript
const { sentryEsbuildPlugin } = require("@sentry/esbuild-plugin");

require("esbuild").build({
  sourcemap: true,
  plugins: [
    sentryEsbuildPlugin({
      org: process.env.SENTRY_ORG,
      project: process.env.SENTRY_PROJECT,
      authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,
    }),
  ],
});
```

## Method 2: sentry-cli

For CI pipelines or build tools without plugin support:

```bash
# Install sentry-cli
npm install @sentry/cli --save-dev
# Or globally
curl -sL https://sentry.io/get-cli/ | sh

# Set environment variables
export SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN="sntrys_..."
export SENTRY_ORG="my-org"
export SENTRY_PROJECT="my-project"

# Inject Debug IDs into source files and maps
sentry-cli sourcemaps inject ./dist

# Upload source maps
sentry-cli sourcemaps upload ./dist
```

With explicit release:

```bash
VERSION=$(sentry-cli releases propose-version)
sentry-cli releases new "$VERSION"
sentry-cli releases set-commits "$VERSION" --auto
sentry-cli sourcemaps inject ./dist
sentry-cli sourcemaps upload --release="$VERSION" ./dist
sentry-cli releases finalize "$VERSION"
sentry-cli deploys new --release="$VERSION" -e production
```

## Method 3: Sentry Wizard

The wizard auto-detects your build tool and configures everything:

```bash
npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i sourcemaps
```

## Auth Token Setup

Create an auth token at `sentry.io/settings/auth-tokens/` with scopes:
- **Project: Read & Write**
- **Release: Admin**

Store as `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable. For CI, add to your pipeline's secrets.

Alternatively, create a `.env.sentry-build-plugin` file (do NOT commit):

```
SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=sntrys_YOUR_TOKEN_HERE
```

## Important: Delete Source Maps After Upload

Source maps contain your original source code. Delete them from your deployment:

```javascript
// In build plugin config
sentryVitePlugin({
  sourcemaps: {
    filesToDeleteAfterUpload: ["./**/*.map"],
  },
});
```

Or use `sourcemap: "hidden"` in your build config to generate maps without `//# sourceMappingURL` references.

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Minified stack traces | Source maps not uploaded | Run upload after build |
| "No source maps found" | Wrong upload path | Verify `./dist` contains `.map` files |
| "Authentication failed" | Bad or expired token | Regenerate at sentry.io/settings/auth-tokens/ |
| Maps uploaded but not applied | Release mismatch | Ensure `release` in `Sentry.init()` matches the upload release |
| "Debug IDs not found" | Inject step skipped | Run `sentry-cli sourcemaps inject` before upload |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Required |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` | Auth token for upload API (secret) | Yes |
| `SENTRY_ORG` | Organization slug | Yes |
| `SENTRY_PROJECT` | Project slug | Yes |
| `SENTRY_RELEASE` | Release version to associate maps with | Recommended |

sections.title: Build tool plugins
sections.body: Build plugins are the recommended upload method. Install `@sentry/vite-plugin`, `@sentry/webpack-plugin`, `@sentry/rollup-plugin`, or `@sentry/esbuild-plugin` as dev dependencies. Configure with `org`, `project`, and `authToken`. The plugin injects Debug IDs into bundles during build and uploads matching source maps to Sentry. The Sentry plugin must be the last plugin in the plugin array to ensure all other transformations are captured. Plugins automatically skip uploads during development/watch mode. Use `sourcemaps.filesToDeleteAfterUpload` to remove `.map` files from your deployment.

sections.title: sentry-cli uploads
sections.body: Use `sentry-cli` for CI pipelines or unsupported build tools. The two-step process is: `sentry-cli sourcemaps inject ./dist` (adds Debug IDs to source files and maps) then `sentry-cli sourcemaps upload ./dist`. For release-based association, wrap with `sentry-cli releases new VERSION` and `sentry-cli releases finalize VERSION`. Key flags: `--release` to specify version, `--dist` for build variants, `--url-prefix '~/'` to match deployed URL paths, `--validate` to verify map validity, and `--strip-common-prefix` to normalize paths.

sections.title: Auth token configuration
sections.body: Source map uploads require a Sentry auth token with 'Project: Read & Write' and 'Release: Admin' scopes. Create tokens at sentry.io/settings/auth-tokens/. Organization-level tokens are preferred over personal tokens for CI. Store as `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable in your CI secrets. Build plugins also accept a `.env.sentry-build-plugin` file in your project root — never commit this file. The token format starts with `sntrys_`.

sections.title: Source map security
sections.body: Source maps contain your original source code including comments and variable names. Never deploy `.map` files to production servers accessible to users. Use `sourcemap: 'hidden'` in your build config to generate maps without `//# sourceMappingURL` comments in the output. Use `filesToDeleteAfterUpload` in build plugins to remove maps after upload. Verify your deployment does not serve `.map` files by checking `https://your-app.com/assets/main.js.map` returns 404.

sections.title: Troubleshooting source maps
sections.body: If stack traces are still minified: (1) verify source maps were uploaded — check Sentry Settings → Source Maps for your release, (2) ensure the `release` in `Sentry.init()` matches the release used during upload, (3) check that Debug IDs are injected — search your built JS files for `//# debugId=`, (4) run `sentry-cli sourcemaps upload --validate` to verify maps are valid, (5) enable `debug: true` in Sentry.init() and check console for upload errors. Timing matters — source maps must be uploaded before the first error is captured for that release.

links.context: Set the release identifier during SDK initialization
The release option must match the upload release
Captured errors use source maps for readable stack traces

related.note: The release set in Sentry.init() must match source map uploads
release option in config determines which source maps apply
Vite builds with @sentry/vite-plugin for automatic uploads

scripts.name: Upload Source Maps
scripts.description: Upload source maps to Sentry using sentry-cli with release management

SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN.description: Sentry auth token with Release:Admin and Project:Read&Write scopes

BUILD_DIR.description: Path to build output directory containing source maps

SENTRY_RELEASE.description: Release version (auto-detected from git if omitted)

buildDir.description: Path to build output with source maps

release.description: Release version that source maps were uploaded to

filesUploaded.description: Number of source map files uploaded

depends.reason: Release identifier in Sentry.init() must match the upload release
